text: The patient is positioned in the prone jackknife position. The surgeon identifies a long fistula tract in the right buttock at the eight o'clock position. The tract is curated, and the external opening is widened. A tube of measured length is inserted into the tract without being sutured to the skin.

During post-operative care, the surgeon removes the tube from the fistula tract before each dressing. The tract is cleaned using a cotton swab mounted on an artery forceps, and the tube is reinserted. As the deeper tract heals, the tube's length is gradually shortened until it is no longer needed.
